Still, in line with the climate lens, a second action is identifying which neighborhoods should be more susceptible to climate hazards.
Although this is not the objective of this study, it is already known that the frequency of ямВoods and landslides in the ABC Paulista is very high, especially in the rainy season.
The ABC region has already mapped the risk areas of each municipality, which are most susceptible to ямВooding and landslides.
This mapping is continually updated, identifying the continuous expansion of risk areas (Instituto de Pesquisas T├йcnicas, 2014; Canil and Nogueira, 2018; Instituto Geol├│gico, 2020).
The causes include heavy rains, unplanned urban occupation, or insuямГcient rainwater drainage network.

However, despite knowing the most susceptible areas and alert measures by the Civil Defense, the impacts are still severe, which conямБrms the gaps in preparation or adaptation in response to extreme events.
Every year in the rainy season, each municipality in ABC Paulista experiences diямАerent impacts during extreme rain events.
The most extreme events recorded so far happened on April 6/7, 2017 (2 h, 90 mm), November 23, 2018 (1h30тАУ84.7 mm), March 10, 2019 (10h: 95.6 mm), and December 28, 2021.
On March 10тАУ11, 2019, the municipalities of SA, SBC and SCS were the most aямАected by the extreme rainfall event.
In Santo Andr├й and S├гo Bernardo do Campo was recorded 95.6 mm and 110 mm, respectively, in 10 h.
ABC region municipalities reported many aямАected streets and neighborhoods and two drowning deaths in SA.
Across the ABC region, 12 deaths were reported, the vast majority due to drowning and a house collapse in Ribeir├гo Pires.
In addition to the deaths, many people were left homeless (Ferreira et al., 2019; Santos et al., 2019).
Given this reality, adaptation plans must be constructed to minimize the impacts of extreme variability, especially rainfall, which already negatively aямАects the most vulnerable population.

For this reason, the methodology proposed by the OECD (Organization for Economic Cooperation and Development) (2009) must be followed, improving the study of the climate threat by evaluating the extremes following the line of the climate lens and then moving on to the other phases, which are: (b) assessment of climate risk, (c) identiямБcation of adaptation and resilience measures, (d) selection and prioritization of measures, (e) implementation, (f) monitoring and evaluation and (g) communication of the plan and its results.
Conclusion National and international eямАorts to mitigate climate change do not have shown any tangible eямАect on global greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions or the practices that cause them.
The ямБght against climate change must be conducted mainly at the local level, that is, as close as possible to the origin of the problem and where its eямАects are felt (Bulkeley and Newell, 2015).

Thus, public policies in councils must advance to reduce vulnerabilities and, consequently, risks, for which the applicability of these policies requires urgency and commitment that involves society and the regionтАЩs population.
Thus, adaptation plans must be an urgent priority in cities.
ABC Paulista can become a pioneer in this regard.
This study was intended to assist in preparing these plans in its ямБrst phase, called тАЬclimate lensтАЭ.

The projections of temperatures (Tmax and Tmin) and precipitation of the Eta-HADGEM2_ES and CNRM-CM6-1-HR models showed a consensus for increasing maximum and minimum temperatures until the 21st century in all municipalities and emission scenarios.
The anomalies projected by Eta-HADGEM2_ES are the largest, projecting an increase of up to 7.4тЧжC in S├гo Caetano do Sul for the RCP8.5 scenario.
The high spatial resolution of this model (5 ├Ч 5 km) should inямВuence the accuracy of the projections.
The CNRM-CM6-1-HR model is one of the highest-resolution models of the CMIP6.

However, it is still coarse compared to the Eta-HADGEM2_ES, and the temperature increase (5.1тЧжC) was more expressive in the counties of SCS and DIA for the SSP5-8.5 scenario for the period 2071тАУ 2100.
However, for precipitation, there was a consensus for reduction by climatic models in all scenarios and time-slices 2020тАУ2040 and 2041тАУ2070.
The municipalities of Diadema (тИТ78.4%) and SCS (тИТ78%) had the most signiямБcant reductions in December for the RCP8.5 scenario.
The CNRM-CM6-1-HR, on the other hand, projected the highest rainfall deямБcit in December (-30.9%) in SCS for the SSP5-8.5 scenario in the 2020тАУ2040 time period.
The TerraClimate database validated with observed data showed overestimations for precipitation and underestimations for temperature.
The validation is essential because these data are widely used as terrestrial truths in regions without observed data.
Hence the importance of data validation, as was also done for climate model simulations.
TerraClimateтАЩs warming scenarios showed a consensus for temperature increase (Tmax and Tmin).
As for precipitation, it showed a reduction for some municipalities (Mau├б тИТ4.7% in spring) and excess for others (Ribeir├гo Pires: 24.8% and Santo Andr├й: 23.7%) for SWL4тЧжC.
Thus, as simulated and projected, the increase in Tmax and Tmin should inямВuence the intensity of extreme heat events in the ABC Paulista region.
On the other hand, the reduction of rainfall in the monthly and annual scale should not mean a decrease in extreme daily events that cause ямВoods and landslides.
However, it leaves an alert of water scarcity for the supply and demand of the population.
It is important to emphasize the uncertainties of models and emission scenarios.
Climate models are invaluable tools, but we must know their limits.
Thus, the projected climate scenarios are directions.
They are not predictions of future events but show the future in diямАerent directions based on driving forces.
If it happens, the decision maker must understand and be prepared to make the best decisions.
Adaptation plans for ABC Paulista are necessary since each municipality identiямБes the hazard in diямАerent proportions and intensities.
These results should contribute to the ямБrst phase of the construction of an adaptation plan, giving a ямБrst vision of the climate threat that should intensify by the end of the 20th century.
